subject: ap-world-history
title: Short Answer Question — AP World History

parts.prompt:
Identify one development that contributed to the spread of gunpowder technologies in the
period before circa 1500.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: The Mongol conquests contributed to the spread of gunpowder technologies.
1 pt: Warfare between states led to the spread of gunpowder technologies.
1 pt: Some states employed foreign military specialists who could train their forces in how to use new types of cannons and other gunpowder weapons.
1 pt: Gunpowder technologies spread along trade routes, such as the Silk Road and the Indian Ocean.

parts.prompt:
Explain one way the use of military technologies affected the development of land-based
empires in the period circa 1450 to 1750.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Muslim empires such as the Ottomans and Mughals used armed trade and gunpowder to expand their territories by conquering weaker neighboring states.
1 pt: Some European states were able to create overseas empires by using cannons and other gunpowder weapons against the technologically less developed societies they encountered.
1 pt: Many states were able to use gunpowder weapons to eliminate or reduce challenges to their authority from the nobility, for example by building or expanding their professional armies.

parts.prompt:
Explain one way navigational technologies contributed to economic change in the period
circa 1450 to 1750.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Improved compass designs enabled Europeans to sail to the Americas, eventually creating the Atlantic system of trade.
1 pt: Knowledge of seasonal monsoon winds allowed European states to establish trading-post empires in the Indian Ocean.
1 pt: Improved astrolabes and better mapmaking techniques facilitated European exploration of the African coast, which led them to set up trading posts on the continent.
